statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.ExecConcatTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName FROM bronze.Product
statements.rationale: Dynamic SQL is built with concatenation before EXEC, so LLM-assisted resolution is required to recover the final statement.

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName FROM bronze.Product
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT ProductID, ProductName FROM bronze.Product ), logical AS ( SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName FROM import ), final AS ( SELECT * FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both statements reference the same source table: bronze.Product

output_columns.summary: Both statements output the same columns: ProductAlternateKey (CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25))) and EnglishProductName (ProductName)

joins.summary: No joins present in either statement

filters.summary: No filters present in either statement

aggregation_grain.summary: Both statements have the same grain - row-by-row selection with no aggregation. The refactored version uses CTEs but produces identical results
